goal: Make structured constraints risks and followups explicit-only instead of default agent seal output

summary.title: Require explicit structured signals
summary.what: Changed the default seal workflow so agents record decisions by default and cannot create new risks, anti-patterns, or follow-ups through the CLI unless they explicitly opt in with --allow-structured-signals. The seal starter now omits those explicit-only fields, risks and followups are no longer daily commands, and Hub no longer promotes the risks page in primary navigation.
summary.why: Agent-authored notes were too easy to promote into long-lived shared facts. Making structured signals explicit-only prevents default seals from polluting future agent context while preserving historical risk, follow-up, constraint, and resolution paths.
summary.user_goal: Make structured constraints risks and followups explicit-only instead of default agent seal output

decisions.chose: Keep decisions and review notes as the default authoring surface, while omitting risks, anti_patterns, and followups from the starter.
decisions.rationale: The fields create long-lived action signals; showing them as empty arrays encourages agents to fill them for completeness.

decisions.chose: Have CLI seal submit reject newly authored risks, anti_patterns, and followups unless --allow-structured-signals is set.
decisions.rationale: This makes seal-time creation an explicit action without deleting historical storage or internal fixture support.

decisions.chose: Move risks and followups out of the daily command group and remove risks.html from Hub primary navigation while keeping the page available for deep links.
decisions.rationale: Existing catalogs and resolution flows remain readable, but the default workflow no longer presents these signals as a normal backlog.

decisions.chose: Update README, specs, AGENTS templates, and Copilot stub to describe decisions as the default seal contract and structured signals as explicit-only.
decisions.rationale: The prompt contract must match the CLI gate so agents are not instructed to write fields the default submit path rejects.

rejected.alternative: Delete risk, followup, and anti_pattern storage entirely
rejected.reason: That would break historical catalogs, eval fixtures, and resolution flows; the safer change is to stop default creation first.

rejected.alternative: Only hide noisy signals in Hub or context output
rejected.reason: That would still allow agent seals to keep polluting the ledger; the user explicitly wanted an entry-control fix.

review_notes: A package-wide go test run was interrupted after more than three minutes without output; targeted affected tests passed.

architectural_claims: Default seal authoring is decision-first; structured risks, constraints, and follow-ups are explicit-only signals.
CLI submit is the default enforcement boundary for agent-authored structured signals.
behavioral_changes: seal_result_starter omits summary.risks, summary.anti_patterns, summary.followups, and summary.acknowledged_constraints by default.
mainline seal --submit rejects summary.risks, summary.anti_patterns, and summary.followups unless --allow-structured-signals is passed through the CLI.
mainline risks and mainline followups appear under advanced help instead of daily commands.
Hub no longer links risks.html from the primary sidebar.

data_model_changes.name: IntentSummary risks/followups JSON encoding
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Existing ledgers with risks/followups still unmarshal; empty new default summaries omit those fields.

migration_notes: Existing risks, followups, anti_patterns, and resolution events remain readable.
Agents that intentionally create new structured signals during seal must use --allow-structured-signals.
